summary: A high-quality implementation-resolved contracts file that faithfully lowers every concept-layer truth (source authority, derived projection, round-trip editing, surfaced conflicts, stable identity, layout-hints-advisory, multi-document workspace) into concrete branded types, discriminated-union protocols, and store slices. The type surface is dense, self-documenting, and leaves minimal interpretive slack. Minor concerns are limited to unexported internal helpers and the co-location of UI-layout state with domain-transport contracts.
strengths: Branded types (StableId, Revision, MutationId, etc.) enforce identity safety across the entire contract surface, directly realizing the concept layer's StableIdentity truth
Discriminated unions for GraphMutationIntent, WsClientEnvelope, and WsServerEnvelope provide exhaustive, self-documenting protocol coverage that maps one-to-one with scenario flows
Clean abstraction progression from primitives → semantic records → graph projection → editing pipeline → HTTP/WS API → store slices mirrors the concept layer's derivation chain without leaking levels

Every concept-layer truth is traceable: SourcePatchPlan enforces round-trip editing, GraphConflict surfaces ambiguity, layoutHints are advisory-only separate from semantic records, WorkspaceSnapshot spans multiple documents
Extract utility types at the bottom (ServerReadyMessage, ProjectionPatchMessage, etc.) provide ergonomic narrowing without duplicating the union definition
weaknesses: No rationale for specific enum choices (e.g., why exactly four GraphEdgeKind variants, why these GraphConflictCode values) — a brief Agentish companion or inline design note would improve activation preload for agents modifying these types

Internal helper types (Message, Mutation, HandleConnection, DraftConnection, EntityRef, HandleRef) are unexported, limiting downstream reuse and forcing duplication if other modules need the same patterns
UiSlice and IoSlice co-located in the same contracts file mixes UI-layout concerns with domain-transport state; splitting into a separate UI-contracts file would sharpen layer boundaries
